item.text: I’ll review the design against the cited programme, feasibility, steering, and literature context, then give a direct verdict with ranked concerns. I’m first loading the project workflow and locating the relevant review files.

item.aggregated_output:
[bd prime] If this output is truncated by your host, read the full persisted hook output before continuing; it may contain project memories and session rules not visible in the preview.

# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when a beads workspace is resolved

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)
- `bd doctor --check=conventions` - Check for convention drift (lint, stale, orphans)

### Quality Tools
- `bd create --validate` - Check description has required sections
- `bd create --acceptance="criteria"` - Set acceptance criteria (checked by --validate)
- `bd create --design="decisions"` - Record design decisions
- `bd create --notes="context"` - Add supplementary notes
- `bd config set validation.on-create warn` - Auto-validate on every create
- `bd lint` - Check existing issues for missing sections

### Lifecycle & Hygiene
- `bd defer <id> --until="date"` - Defer work to a future date
- `bd supersede <id> --with=<new-id>` - Mark issue as superseded
- `bd close <id> --suggest-next` - Show newly unblocked issues after closing
- `bd stale` - Find issues with no recent activity
- `bd orphans` - Find issues with broken dependencies
- `bd preflight` - Pre-PR checks (lint, stale, orphans)
- `bd human <id>` - Flag for human decision (list/respond/dismiss)

### Structured Workflows
- `bd formula list` - See available workflow templates
- `bd mol pour <name>` - Start structured workflow from formula

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

     1	# P3-E-DECONF-0 — DECONF/1: the aligned-generic-store deconfound (experiment design)
     2	
     3	> **Status: [EXP-DESIGN] deliverable of the round-1 steering (docs/next/analysis/
     4	> round1-steering.md, "run the cheap decisive kills now" item (b), bead-to-be
     5	> P3-E-DECONF-0). Nothing here is frozen, pre-registered, scheduled, or run; no
     6	> verdict, audit, frozen object, or registered ruling is touched. The design's
     7	> assumption entries are REGISTERED in the fresh append-only block
     8	> **ASM-0960…ASM-0966** (block ASM-0960–0979 assigned to this bead; 0967–0979
     9	> remain free).** Author: Fable, chief-architect role (`kern/fable-designer`),
    10	> 2026-07-11. This design goes through the standing external (GPT-5.6-class)
    11	> review before any prereg freeze.
    12	>
    13	> **Provenance of the question.** Both independent round-1 subjective analyses
    14	> named, in nearly identical words, the programme's single biggest risk: *the
    15	> kernel's distinctive semantics contribute nothing beyond what a generic typed
    16	> store + executor + strong tool/RAG baseline already provide* — and both named
    17	> the **aligned-generic-store deconfound** the highest-value early experiment
    18	> (docs/next/analysis/round1-fable-subjective.md §3.1 item 2;
    19	> poc/gpt56-review/round1-subjective/last-message.json §3 item 2 and closing
    20	> lines; synthesised in round1-steering.md convergence items 5 and the
    21	> recommended-kills list). This document is that experiment.
    22	>
    23	> **Blocked-by inputs, read in full at source:** docs/next/analysis/
    24	> round1-steering.md; docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md (§0–§2, §5);
    25	> registry/experiments/f2b-replicate.json + registry/assessments/f2b-replicate
    26	> does_not_license (as restated in the synthesis §2); registry/experiments/
    27	> f2b-transfer.json + poc/f2b-transfer/judge-1-results/stage1-analysis.json;
    28	> registry/experiments/knull.json (FROZEN v1) + knull-v2.json (DRAFT);
    29	> poc/f2b/runner/f2b_runner.py (the pinned mechanism source);
    30	> docs/next/design/FRONT.md §5 (ASM-0853 matched-retrieval rules);
    31	> docs/next/design/RAGC.md (arm inventory GR-A/GR-C/GR-D, ASM-0920–0927);
    32	> docs/next/design/MF0.md §8.5 (framework-owned content-type cells, ASM-0891);
    33	> docs/next/lit/RAG.md (P3-LR-RAG).
    34	>
    35	> **Tag convention (house discipline):** `[MEASURED: ref]` = a programme registry
    36	> verdict/assessment/artifact restated strictly inside its envelope;
    37	> `[LIT-BACKED: lit-review §]` = an external fact verified at primary source by a
    38	> completed Phase-0 lit review, cited through that review; `[STIPULATED: id]` = a
    39	> design choice made here or inherited from a registered stipulation;
    40	> `[EXTRAPOLATION: id]` = a registered forward projection, never a premise.
    41	
    42	---
    43	
    44	## 0. Contract: the question, split where the mechanism splits it
    45	
    46	The confound to cut, in the registry's own words: the one audited end-task
    47	positive is *correct-alignment-specific, not shown kernel-content-specific* — "a
    48	generic aligned answer-key + retry would reproduce it" — and its de-confound is
    49	unrun.
    50	
    51	- PREMISE: f2b-replicate PASSED — SmolLM2-135M + kernel-verify-retry (k=4) beats
    52	  SmolLM2-1.7B-alone by +0.1507 absolute (one-sided 95% BCa LB +0.1053) at
    53	  cost_ratio_vs_R3 = 0.103; the seed-pinned derangement recovers ~0 of the lift
    54	  (point −0.021); the kernel arm beat gloss-self-verify (0.4893), the trained
    55	  Skywork PRM-1.5B (0.5267), and the passive kernel-as-text null (0.4920)
    56	  [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/f2b-replicate.json, audit CONFIRMED, restated per
    57	  feasibility-synthesis §2].
    58	- PREMISE: the lift's attribution ceiling is set by its own assessment — the
    59	  verifier accepts iff the answer string-equals the canonical record while gold
    60	  was DEFINED by that same equality, so the shuffled control provably cannot
    61	  discriminate NSM content from correct record↔item alignment; nothing measured
    62	  distinguishes the kernel store from ANY typed store + checker
    63	  [MEASURED: registry/assessments/f2b-replicate.json does_not_license, adopted
    64	  verbatim by feasibility-synthesis §2].
    65	- PREMISE: the f2b-transfer stage-1 endorsement gate has just READ OUT and
    66	  PASSED: blind external adjudication endorses the membership gold at
    67	  A = 0.9784 (317/324 resolved pairs; one-sided 95% Wilson LB 0.9606 ≥ 0.70 bar;
    68	  raw two-judge agreement 0.90; n_adjudicated 360; unresolved 36 ≤ 15% cap;
    69	  adjudication_valid = true) — the definitional-circularity kill (d) did NOT
    70	  fire, stage 2 is licensed, and 324 externally-labelled kernel-covered items
    71	  now EXIST as a pinned scoring asset
    72	  [MEASURED: poc/f2b-transfer/judge-1-results/stage1-analysis.json;
    73	  registry/experiments/f2b-transfer.json stage-1 endorsement gate; the stage-1
    74	  results-log append is runner-role work in flight].
    75	
    76	The deconfound question — *is the lift kernel-specific or
    77	generic-aligned-store-structural?* — decomposes along the measured mechanism
    78	into two channels that need different experiments, only one of which is blocked:
    79	
    80	**Channel 1 — the RUNTIME channel (this experiment).** In the verify-retry
    81	topology the store content NEVER enters any prompt: the retry loop is
    82	answer → deterministic check → reject → resample, and the store acts *only*
    83	through `verifier.check()`, a pure normalised-string-equality / set-membership
    84	function over three fields of the canonical record (definition text, term
    85	label, claim list).
    86	
    87	- PREMISE: the above is a code-level fact of the pinned harness —
    88	  `run_verify_retry` calls `answer_once` (prompt = frames + item only, no
    89	  record bytes) then `verify_answer` (IF-C extract then `verifier.check`), and
    90	  the knull map M-V measured 3456/3456 verifier decisions bitwise-identical
    91	  after stripping all non-gloss record fields [MEASURED:
    92	  poc/f2b/runner/f2b_runner.py at the f2b-replicate harness pin (functions
    93	  build_prompt, answer_once, run_verify_retry, verify_answer,
    94	  KernelVerifier.check); registry/experiments/knull.json map M-V assumption].
    95	
    96	So the runtime question is not "does NSM content help the model" — the model
    97	never sees it — but "does the check function read ANYTHING a generic aligned
    98	store lacks". That is decidable by exhaustive enumeration on a CPU, at ~$0
    99	(§3).
   100	
   101	**Channel 2 — the AUTHORED-CONTENT channel (knull-v2's, NOT this experiment).**
   102	When surfaces are REGENERATED from each store (items and answers re-rendered
   103	from NSM vs plain-dictionary vs nonce content), store content does reach the
   104	model through the items themselves; that is the knull/knull-v2 K-NULL ablation
   105	— FROZEN (v1) / DRAFT (v2), currently blocked on the maintainer-ratified
   106	plain-arm quality-gate rewrite [MEASURED: registry/experiments/knull.json
   107	status FROZEN + knull-v2.json status DRAFT; STIPULATED: ASM-0700/ASM-0703 the
   108	ruling]. DECONF deliberately does NOT touch that channel, does not amend
   109	knull/knull-v2, and is runnable NOW precisely because its generic store
   110	requires ZERO authored prose (§2).
   111	
   112	- DECISION: DECONF/1 is therefore staged cheap-first as three cells —
   113	  **A1** (CPU, ~$0): the aligned-generic-store extensional-equivalence audit on
   114	  the f2b verify-retry mechanism; **A2** (CPU, ~$0): the generic-typed-store +
   115	  generic-executor reproduction of the a5-llm engine leg (the conventional-
   116	  substrate control that assessment names as missing); **B** (single small GPU
   117	  run, ≤ ~2 GPU-h / ≤ ~$15): the strong generic tool/RAG baseline through the
   118	  IDENTICAL verify-retry topology on the SAME externally-adjudicated items,
   119	  isolating alignment itself as the last unshared ingredient. Claim licences,
   120	  gates and does-not-amend clauses per the registered contract
   121	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0960].
   122	
   123	Ownership map (no duplication):
   124	
   125	| Cell | Factorial owner | DECONF's relation |
   126	|---|---|---|
   127	| A1 aligned generic answer-key store | MF0 §8.5 content-type cell (e) "aligned-non-kernel store" (ASM-0891) | DECONF instantiates the STRUCTURE-MINIMAL member (opaque-string projection, zero authoring); knull-v2 owns the authored-content members (plain/nonce) |
   128	| A2 generic executor | RAGC GR-C (ASM-0924) | DECONF runs the right-sized precursor on the pinned a5-llm slice; the full manifest cell supersedes it when built |
   129	| B generic RAG + matched retry | RAGC GR-A / GR-D (ASM-0920/0924) | DECONF runs right-sized precursors at the f2b scope, adopting the FRONT/1 §5 parity mechanics (ASM-0853) that apply; superseded by the RAGC manifest cells at any G4/W1 use |
   130	
   131	Gate placement: A1/A2 are census/diagnostic-class (G1-class, CPU, no model);
   132	B is a G2-class attribution experiment on the covered slice — no cell here is a
   133	W1 claim, nothing is NLB-gated (no natural-language input exists anywhere in
   134	this design), and no cell licenses any competitiveness sentence
   135	[STIPULATED: ASM-0960].
   136	
   137	---
   138	
   139	## 1. The mechanism fact the whole design leans on (stated once, precisely)
   140	
   141	For every arm f2b-replicate and f2b-transfer run or plan, the pipeline is a
   142	deterministic function of (models, items, seeds, store) in which the store is
   143	consulted ONLY through `check(record_extracted_from_answer, item)` — accept /
   144	reject / abstain — and through nothing else: no store bytes in any prompt, no
   145	store-dependent decoding, no store-dependent seed arithmetic [MEASURED:
   146	poc/f2b/runner/f2b_runner.py at the pinned f2b-replicate harness manifest —
   147	build_prompt takes (frames, item, context_docs) and the verify arms pass no
   148	context_docs; resampling is det_u over (arm, item id, seed, attempt) only].
   149	
   150	Consequence (a determinism statement, not a hypothesis): if a substitute store
   151	S′ yields the SAME check() decision as the kernel store on every (item,
   152	admissible answer) pair, then every output, every per-item correctness bit,
   153	every endpoint, and every verdict of every run of this topology — past AND
   154	future, membership-gold or external-gold — is invariant under kernel→S′
   155	substitution. The deconfound therefore does not need to re-run any model to
   156	decide the runtime channel: it needs to measure decision concordance over the
   157	FULL reachable decision space, which is finite and small (closed answer sets:
   158	≤4 options or yes/no per item). That is Stage A1.
   159	
   160	---
   161	
   162	## 2. The generic stores (all derivation, no authoring)
   163	
   164	### 2.1 GS-A — the aligned generic answer-key store (Stage A1/B)
   165	
   166	- DECISION: GS-A is a **mechanical field projection** of the pinned kernel-v0 /
   167	  molecules-v0 store onto exactly the read-set of `check()`: per covered
   168	  concept, the four-column row `{concept_id, term_label(s), canonical_text,
   169	  claims[]}` — every byte copied verbatim from the kernel's own records, all
   170	  NSM structure (explication trees, primes, vectors, types, provenance frames,
   171	  engine hooks) DELETED. Derivation is one deterministic seed-free script,
   172	  content-hashed; the row set is coverage-identical and alignment-identical to
   173	  the kernel store by construction (same record_path keys the items already
   174	  pin) [STIPULATED: ASM-0961].
   175	- Why this is runnable today when knull-v2 is not: GS-A **authors nothing** —
   176	  it contains no new prose, so the maintainer language-quality gate on authored
   177	  control stores (the knull plain-arm ruling) has no object here; the
   178	  quality-gate applies to authored aligned stores (knull-v2's plain arm), not
   179	  to a byte-projection of the kernel's own strings [STIPULATED: ASM-0961; the
   180	  ruling itself is ASM-0700/ASM-0703, untouched].
   181	- What GS-A operationalises: the exact phrase both reviews used — "a plain
   182	  aligned answer-key". It is the STRUCTURE-MINIMAL member of the MF0 §8.5
   183	  content-type family (cell (e) direction): same coverage, same alignment, none
   184	  of the kernel's semantics [STIPULATED: ASM-0961].
   185	
   186	### 2.2 GS-C — the generic typed store + generic executor (Stage A2)
   187	
   188	- DECISION: the a5 code-vertical typed world (the no-LLM deterministically
   189	  extracted records the a5 engine runs on) loaded into the RAGC-pinned generic
   190	  deterministic engine — SQLite with a pinned recursive-CTE query library (the
   191	  GR-C default, ASM-0924) — exposing `lookup / neighbors / check` over the same
   192	  typed content; query library version-pinned and content-hashed
   193	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0963, adopting the ASM-0924 executor default].
   194	
   195	### 2.3 GS-R — the strong generic tool/RAG store (Stage B)
   196	
   197	- DECISION: BM25 over the pinned common text serialisation of the SAME store
   198	  records (the existing deterministic renderer — the serialisation ASM-0922
   199	  pins), index bytes charged, retrieved context injected through the harness's
   200	  existing context_docs path at a pinned retrieved-token cap identical across
   201	  arms; BM25 is the mandatory lexical cell and a dense embedder is NOT added at
   202	  this right-size (disclosed; the RAGC manifest owns the dense cell)
   203	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0964; LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §6.2 — BM25 as the robust zero-shot
   204	  lexical baseline, BEIR, arXiv:2104.08663, NeurIPS 2021].
   205	
   206	---
   207	
   208	## 3. Stage A1 — the extensional-equivalence audit (the cheapest falsifier, ~$0, CPU)
   209	
   210	### 3.1 Procedure
   211	
   212	- DECISION: enumerate the FULL decision grid — for each of the three pinned
   213	  item corpora (d-qa 650, d-qa-r 1000, d-qa-t 360; corpus hashes as pinned in
   214	  the f2b-replicate / f2b-transfer records) × every admissible answer in the
   215	  item's closed answer space (each MC option; yes and no for claim items) ×
   216	  both verifier variants (true map; the seed-pinned shuffled derangement,
   217	  replayed with the same recorded permutation applied to GS-A rows) — compute
   218	  the decision triple (extract_ok, decidable, consistent) under (i) the pinned
   219	  KernelVerifier over the kernel store and (ii) the same checker class over
   220	  GS-A. Grid size ≤ ~16k decision pairs (2,010 items × ≤4 answers × 2
   221	  variants); pure string ops; minutes on the local box at nice -n 10
   222	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0962].
   223	
   224	### 3.2 Metric and decision rule
   225	
   226	- DECISION: the primary statistic is **C_dec = concordant decision triples /
   227	  total grid size** — an exhaustive COUNT over the full reachable decision
   228	  space, so it carries no sampling error and no confidence interval; the
   229	  pre-registered reading [STIPULATED: ASM-0962]:
   230	  - **C_dec = 1.0 ⇒ verdict-input KERNEL-RUNTIME-GENERIC (the deflationary
   231	    half).** By §1 determinism, the generic aligned answer-key store reproduces
   232	    every F2-line endpoint VERBATIM: the reproduction statistic the steering
   233	    asked for is `R_repro = lift(GS-A) / lift(kernel) ≡ 1.0` identically — the
   234	    measured +0.1507, its CIs, the shuffled-control readings, and every future
   235	    output of the same topology (including f2b-transfer stage-2's external-gold
   236	    endpoints when they run) are bit-for-bit invariant under kernel → GS-A
   237	    substitution. The kernel-SPECIFIC runtime claim on the F2 line is dead at
   238	    this scope, at zero model spend.
   239	  - **C_dec < 1.0 ⇒ a runtime kernel channel EXISTS.** Every discordant (item,
   240	    answer, variant) triple is emitted and classified by check-path (def-match
   241	    / term-match / claim / shuffled-composition); a discordance lying on a
   242	    trajectory reachable by any logged run additionally raises a flag on the
   243	    f2b-replicate interpretation (flag only — the frozen verdict is untouched).
   244	    The discordance classes become the measured definition of "what the kernel
   245	    semantics do at runtime" and Stage B adds the kernel-verify arm back
   246	    (§5.1's conditional arm) so the channel is priced end-to-end.
   247	- Falsifiability, stated honestly: C_dec = 1.0 is the EXPECTED outcome — the
   248	  strip is a projection onto check()'s read-set, and the knull M-V map already
   249	  measured the vector/AST fields inert (3456/3456) [MEASURED:
   250	  registry/experiments/knull.json map M-V]. The residual falsification room is
   251	  real but narrow: the term-for-definition path (`shown_definition`), the
   252	  claim-polarity membership path, the shuffled-map composition, and any
   253	  unnoticed engine consultation inside the checker would each surface as
   254	  discordance. The registered expectation is EXTRAPOLATION ASM-0966
   255	  (non-load-bearing, resolved by the run itself); the VALUE of the stage does
   256	  not depend on surprise — see §3.3.
   257	
   258	### 3.3 Why this is worth registering even if the outcome is expected
   259	
   260	The assessment's circularity reading is currently an ANALYTIC argument attached
   261	to a does_not_license note; programme discipline treats analytic readings and
   262	measured facts differently, and two external reviews have now demanded this
   263	deconfound be EXECUTED rather than argued (round1-steering item 5; the
   264	GPT-5.6 subjective analysis: "the cheap aligned-store control should not
   265	wait"). Stage A1 converts "a generic aligned answer-key WOULD reproduce it"
   266	into "a generic aligned answer-key DOES reproduce it, bit-for-bit, measured
   267	over the exhaustive decision space" — a registered, citable, audit-checkable
   268	fact that (a) every future F2-line claim must carry (§7), (b) re-scopes the
   269	headline language mechanically (§6), and (c) does so without spending the GPU
   270	budget, the human-annotation queue, or the knull quality-gate path that block
   271	every other deconfound [STIPULATED: ASM-0962].
   272	
   273	---
   274	
   275	## 4. Stage A2 — the generic-executor reproduction of the a5-llm engine leg (~$0, CPU)
   276	
   277	- PREMISE: a5-llm PASSED as a substrate comparison — deterministic engine
   278	  conj 1.0 vs best gate-valid LLM cell 0.3398 (+0.6602, LB +0.6346) at
   279	  cost_ratio_min 22,836× — and its own assessment caps the reading: there was
   280	  NO conventional-substrate arm, so "nothing distinguishes the kot-axiom kernel
   281	  from ANY typed store + checker" [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/a5-llm.json PASS
   282	  and registry/assessments/a5-llm.json does_not_license, restated per
   283	  feasibility-synthesis §1].
   284	- DECISION: Stage A2 supplies that missing arm at its cheapest honest size: the
   285	  pinned 977-query kot-query-code/1 slice is run against GS-C (§2.2 — the same
   286	  typed world in SQLite + pinned query library), CPU-only, and read on two
   287	  endpoints: **A_exec** = exact-output agreement with the kot-axiom engine's
   288	  answers on the same queries, and **acc_generic** = correctness against the
   289	  pinned slice gold; per-query wall-clock co-reported (no cost claim tested)
   290	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0963].
   291	- DECISION: decision rule — A_exec ≥ 0.99 AND acc_generic point-equal to the
   292	  engine's covered accuracy at the slice's resolution ⇒ verdict-input
   293	  ENGINE-GENERIC-AT-SCOPE: the a5-llm PASS's licensed sentence is confirmed AS
   294	  its ceiling ("a deterministic engine over its own extracted typed records…"),
   295	  now with the generic-substrate arm measured rather than argued; the kernel
   296	  ENGINE's distinctive contribution on this slice is bounded by the (1 −
   297	  A_exec) residual, which is enumerated and classified (expressiveness gaps in
   298	  SQL/CTE vs engine semantics are the interesting class and are reported as
   299	  such, not hidden). A_exec materially < 0.99 ⇒ the engine does measurable work
   300	  a generic executor does not reproduce — the first positive
   301	  engine-distinctiveness datum in the registry, worth its own follow-up
   302	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0963]. Failure-shape honesty: an UNDER-BUILT query library
   303	  fakes engine-distinctiveness, so A2 inherits the anti-weak-control counter —
   304	  the library is built to reproduce the QUERY GRAMMAR spec, its full source is
   305	  published with the readout, and any non-reproduced class is checked against
   306	  the grammar before being credited to "engine semantics"
   307	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0963, mirroring the ASM-0922 anti-weak-control rule].
   308	
   309	---
   310	
   311	## 5. Stage B — the strong generic baseline through the identical topology, on external gold (GPU, right-sized)
   312	
   313	Stage A1 settles what the ALIGNED generic store does (everything). The live
   314	question it cannot settle: is the ALIGNMENT itself the load-bearing ingredient,
   315	or would a strong generic tool/RAG baseline — same snapshot, same budgets, NO
   316	item-aligned key — reproduce the lift too? f2b-replicate never ran a generic-RAG
   317	arm [MEASURED: registry/experiments/f2b-replicate.json dropped_arms — RAG
   318	dropped at right-size; the F2 confirmatory readings stand at F2's scope], and
   319	both reviews name that the missing first-class rival.
   320	
   321	### 5.1 Arms (all at rung R1 = the pinned SmolLM2-135M revision; 250-item d-qa-t eval prefix; 3 seeds; k = 4)
   322	
   323	| Arm | Definition | Cell lineage |
   324	|---|---|---|
   325	| R1-alone | attempt-0 only, no store, no retrieval | the shared floor |
   326	| GS-A-verify-retry | the §2.1 generic store behind the UNCHANGED verify-retry loop | MF0 cell-(e) structure-minimal member; equals the kernel arm bit-for-bit iff A1 C_dec = 1.0 on d-qa-t |
   327	| GR-A-lite (RAG-alone) | BM25 top-j rendered records injected via context_docs, attempt-0 | RAGC GR-A precursor |
   328	| GR-D-lite (RAG + generic retry) | GR-A-lite wrapped in the SAME loop shape — same k, same resample rule, same abstain-on-exhaustion — with the pinned GENERIC acceptance signal (the harness's existing gloss-self-check over the RETRIEVED text, i.e. acceptance consults retrieval, never the aligned key) | RAGC GR-D precursor |
   329	| kernel-verify-retry (CONDITIONAL) | added back ONLY if A1 found any reachable discordance on d-qa-t; otherwise NOT run — the GS-A arm carries both readings under measured identity | fail-closed conditional |
   330	
   331	- DECISION: scoring — every endpoint on the pinned blind external-adjudication
   332	  gold (the d-adj-t labels stage 1 just produced), item-for-item the same
   333	  250-item externally-labelled eval prefix, seeds, decoding, prompt frames, and
   334	  token discipline as the frozen f2b-transfer stage-2 design, so the two
   335	  experiments' cells are diagnostically comparable (comparability is a labelled
   336	  DIAGNOSTIC, never verdict-bearing across records); DECONF-B consumes NO
   337	  logged cell of any frozen record — every arm is a fresh run under this
   338	  design's own (future) prereg [STIPULATED: ASM-0964].
   339	- DECISION: parity mechanics, adopted right-sized from the registered rules
   340	  rather than re-invented: one retrieved-token cap identical across arms with j
   341	  floating to fill it (ASM-0923's token-not-object matching); generator
   342	  checkpoint / decoding / stop discipline / abstention semantics identical
   343	  across arms with only the evidence slot varying (ASM-0924); BM25 index bytes
   344	  and build compute reported on the arm's ledger row (ASM-0853 Rule 5 / ASM-0925
   345	  shape). Deviations from the full RAGC inventory (no dense cell, no PS/RD
   346	  perturbation cells, no GR-B/GR-C arm here) are DISCLOSED right-sizing, and
   347	  every such cell remains owned by the RAGC manifest for any later G4 use
   348	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0964].
   349	
   350	### 5.2 Endpoints, statistics, decision rules
   351	
   352	- DECISION: instrument gates first, all inherited shapes: P10 extraction gate;
   353	  the RT-7a engagement gate on every retry arm (decidable_fraction ≥ 0.95 for
   354	  the GS-A arm; attempt-0 rejection in [0.05, 0.95]; ≥1 final ≠ attempt-0);
   355	  headroom gate acc_ext(R1-alone) ≤ 0.85; plus the **bridge gate** (knull's
   356	  pattern): lift_ext(GS-A-verify-retry over R1-alone) one-sided 95% BCa LB
   357	  above +0.05 — if the aligned-store lift does not reproduce on external gold
   358	  there is nothing to attribute and the verdict is INSTRUMENT-INVALID-at-B
   359	  (Stage A1's result stands regardless; and if f2b-transfer stage-2 has read
   360	  out by then, a bridge failure here against a stage-2 success is a flagged
   361	  inconsistency to investigate, not silently absorbed) [STIPULATED: ASM-0964].
   362	- DECISION: primary endpoint — **recovery_RAG = lift_ext(GR-D-lite) /
   363	  lift_ext(GS-A-verify-retry)**, both lifts over the shared R1-alone floor,
   364	  paired item-level BCa bootstrap (B = 10,000, PRNG seed pinned at freeze), with
   365	  the pre-registered readings [STIPULATED: ASM-0964]:
   366	  - **recovery_RAG point ≥ 0.70** ⇒ ATTRIBUTION-COLLAPSE-AT-SCOPE: the strong
   367	    generic baseline reproduces the lift WITHOUT the aligned key — even
   368	    alignment is not the distinctive ingredient; this is the direct measured
   369	    input to the K-P3v2(4) attribution-collapse kill at this scope, and the
   370	    F2-line efficiency story survives only as "retrieval + retry at matched
   371	    budget".
   372	  - **recovery_RAG one-sided 95% BCa UB < 0.30 AND Δ_align =
   373	    lift_ext(GS-A-verify-retry) − lift_ext(GR-D-lite) LB > 0** ⇒
   374	    ALIGNMENT-SPECIFIC: the lift requires the item-aligned deterministic
   375	    acceptance; combined with A1 (the aligned store is kernel-agnostic), the
   376	    licensed sentence becomes exactly: *verify-retry against an item-aligned
   377	    deterministic answer key lifts a 135M host on this slice; the kernel is one
   378	    way to AUTHOR such a key; no kernel-specific runtime contribution is
   379	    measured* — routing all remaining kernel value to the authoring/economics
   380	    legs (knull-v2 content, A-F0 mint cost).
   381	  - **between** ⇒ PARTIAL, reported with both bounds, no collapse call either
   382	    way; secondary readout GR-A-lite (retrieval alone, no retry) prices how
   383	    much of any recovery is retrieval vs loop shape (the GR-D − GR-A contrast
   384	    of ASM-0926, right-sized).
   385	- DECISION: reporting requirements — full contrast vector (never the headline
   386	  alone), retrieval hit@j against the item's pinned record co-reported for the
   387	  RAG arms, per-type breakdown descriptive-only, every verdict sentence naming
   388	  the arm that licenses it (the ASM-0926 discipline, right-sized)
   389	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0964].
   390	
   391	### 5.3 What Stage B deliberately does not measure
   392	
   393	No R3/1.7B arm (the efficiency non-inferiority against R3 on external gold is
   394	f2b-transfer's frozen endpoint, not re-litigated here); no PRM arm (HC3 closed
   395	at its size class in f2b-replicate); no NL input anywhere (all items are the
   396	kernel-rendered templated surfaces — the NL wall is l3a-parse/a5-nl territory
   397	and untouched); no coverage claim (all items are kernel-covered by
   398	construction; the mandatory disclosure rides every readout: kernel-
   399	expressibility coverage 0.3542 at rung molecules-v0, measured by m0b on one
   400	incomplete kernel-v0 instance, NOT general coverage) [STIPULATED: ASM-0964].
   401	
   402	---
   403	
   404	## 6. Verdict semantics for the whole experiment (equal prominence, pre-registered)
   405	
   406	- DECISION: DECONF/1's verdict object carries THREE independent readings, one
   407	  per stage, each with its licensed sentence and its kill wiring; no stage's
   408	  outcome is promoted into another's scope [STIPULATED: ASM-0965]:
   409	
   410	| Stage outcome | Verdict input | Licensed consequence (mandatory narration) |
   411	|---|---|---|
   412	| A1 C_dec = 1.0 | KERNEL-RUNTIME-GENERIC | every F2-line claim henceforth carries the invariance lemma: "the store's runtime role is an aligned deterministic answer key; all measured F2-line endpoints are invariant under replacement of the kernel store by its four-column projection"; headline language re-scopes to "aligned deterministic answer-key + retry" pending knull-v2 on the content channel |
   413	| A1 C_dec < 1.0 | KERNEL-RUNTIME-CHANNEL-FOUND | discordance classes published; f2b-line interpretation flagged where reachable; Stage B runs the conditional kernel arm |
   414	| A2 A_exec ≥ 0.99 | ENGINE-GENERIC-AT-SCOPE | the a5-llm does_not_license ceiling is measured, not argued; a5-llm narration unchanged (it already claims no more) but now closed against the missing-control objection |
   415	| A2 A_exec < 0.99 | ENGINE-DISTINCTIVE-RESIDUAL | the residual class is the first measured engine-specific runtime datum; follow-up design bead recommended |
   416	| B recovery_RAG ≥ 0.70 (point) | ATTRIBUTION-COLLAPSE-AT-SCOPE | K-P3v2(4) input at this scope; F2-line survives only as generic retrieval + retry |
   417	| B recovery UB < 0.30 ∧ Δ_align LB > 0 | ALIGNMENT-SPECIFIC | the deconfound's constructive outcome: value = the aligned key; kernel = one authoring route; runtime distinctiveness: none measured (from A1) |
   418	| B bridge/instrument gate failure | INSTRUMENT-INVALID-at-B | nothing attributed; A1/A2 stand; inconsistency with any landed f2b-transfer stage-2 read is flagged for investigation |
   419	
   420	- DECISION: the deflationary outcomes get EQUAL prominence with any positive —
   421	  the knull discipline verbatim; and the rescue paths for kernel-specific value
   422	  are named IN the verdict so the outcome cannot be spun: (i) authored-content
   423	  superiority (knull-v2 PASS-CONTENT), (ii) authoring economics (A-F0), (iii)
   424	  any A1/A2 discordance residual. None of these is licensed by DECONF; each is
   425	  where DECONF's deflationary outcome says to look next
   426	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0965].
   427	
   428	---
   429	
   430	## 7. How DECONF slots into KOT-FAIR/2
   431	
   432	1. **The invariance lemma as a standing rider.** On A1 = 1.0, the lemma of §6
   433	   row 1 attaches to every F2-line consuming claim exactly as the coverage
   434	   disclosure does today — a mandatory one-line rider, enforced at readout
   435	   review, until and unless a later measurement (knull-v2 content channel, an
   436	   A1 re-run after any store/checker version change) modifies it. Any change to
   437	   the checker or store schema invalidates the lemma and requires an A1 re-run
   438	   (the audit is minutes of CPU, so this is cheap insurance, mirroring the
   439	   encoder content-hash discipline) [STIPULATED: ASM-0965].
   440	2. **K-P3v2(4) wiring.** Stage B's contrasts are the right-sized first instance
   441	   of the S − GR-A / S − GR-D contrast family that ASM-0926 wires into the
   442	   attribution-collapse kill; DECONF feeds that kill AT THIS SCOPE only — the
   443	   programme-level kill still requires the RAGC manifest cells at G4
   444	   [STIPULATED: ASM-0965].
   445	3. **Relation to the frozen F2 line.** DECONF amends nothing: f2b-replicate's
   446	   PASS, f2b-transfer's frozen design and stage-1 PASS, knull's frozen record
   447	   and knull-v2's draft all stand byte-identical; DECONF adds the deconfound
   448	   readings their own assessments called for [STIPULATED: ASM-0960].
   449	4. **Relation to the RAGC/FRONT machinery.** DECONF's cells are declared
   450	   right-sized PRECURSORS: they adopt the applicable registered parity rules
   451	   (ASM-0853/0923/0924/0925/0926) without waiting for the manifest
   452	   infrastructure, per the steering's freeze-scaffolding/measure-now
   453	   directive; when the RAGC manifest lands, its cells supersede these for all
   454	   G4/W1 use and DECONF's readings remain valid at their own scope
   455	   [STIPULATED: ASM-0960].
   456	
   457	---
   458	
   459	## 8. Compute plan (cheap-first, worked)
   460	
   461	Planning estimates, never measurements [STIPULATED: ASM-0964]:
   462	
   463	- **A1: ~$0.** ≤ ~16k decision-pair evaluations of pure string ops + one
   464	  4-column store projection script; local box, nice -n 10, minutes to hours
   465	  including harness plumbing; no model, no GPU, no API. Runnable the day the
   466	  (post-review) prereg freezes.
   467	- **A2: ~$0 compute; the cost is engineering.** SQLite + a pinned CTE query
   468	  library over the a5 typed world; 977 queries execute in seconds; the query
   469	  library is roughly a day of implementation and is itself a published,
   470	  reviewable artifact (the anti-weak-control requirement).
   471	- **B: ≤ ~2 GPU-h, ≤ ~$15, Modal, one container.** ~250 items × 3 seeds ×
   472	  (1 + ≤5 + 1 + ≤10) generations/item across the four arms at 135M-class
   473	  option-scoring throughput; BM25 build is CPU. Co-schedulable with the
   474	  f2b-transfer stage-2 campaign (same image, same corpus assets) without
   475	  touching that record's run; inside the standing free-pool/Tier-0
   476	  authorization, with prereg-freeze + runner-role separation (designer never
   477	  runs/grades) exactly as knull pins it.
   478	- Sequencing: A1 → (A2 ∥ B). A1 first because its outcome decides B's
   479	  conditional kernel arm and licenses B's arm-sharing economy; A2 is
   480	  independent and can fill CPU time while B waits for its freeze.
   481	
   482	---
   483	
   484	## 9. Honest limits of this design
   485	
   486	1. **A1 can only close the runtime channel.** A C_dec = 1.0 outcome says
   487	   nothing about authored-content value (knull-v2's question), authoring
   488	   economics (A-F0), consumption channels (A-E2), or any future architecture
   489	   whose coupling is not answer→check→resample. The lemma it licenses is
   490	   scoped to THIS checker/store version pair by content hash.
   491	2. **A1's expected outcome is known-direction.** Registered as EXTRAPOLATION
   492	   ASM-0966 (non-load-bearing); the stage's value is conversion of an analytic
   493	   claim into a measured, riding, re-checkable fact — §3.3 — not surprise.
   494	3. **A2's generic executor can be under-built.** The counter is publication of
   495	   the full query library + grammar-conformance check before any
   496	   "engine-distinctive" credit; residuals are classified, not headline-ified.
   497	4. **B is one retriever, one acceptance signal, one vertical, 250 items.**
   498	   "No generic signal reproduces it" is NOT in B's licence space — only "BM25
   499	   retrieval + the pinned generic self-check at matched budget does not";
   500	   the RAGC menu owns the wider claim. B's external-gold scoring inherits the
   501	   d-adj-t protocol's own limits (single human judge-1 + fallback judge
   502	   sourcing as disclosed in the f2b-transfer record).
   503	5. **Cross-record comparability is diagnostic only.** Bit-level agreement
   504	   between DECONF-B's GS-A arm and f2b-transfer stage-2's kernel arm is
   505	   expected under A1 identity but can be broken by cross-container numeric
   506	   nondeterminism; it is read as a diagnostic, never as an endpoint.
   507	6. **Thresholds (0.99, 0.70/0.30, +0.05 bridge, token caps) are stipulated
   508	   planning values**, maintainer-adjustable at prereg freeze; the 0.30/0.70
   509	   recovery bounds deliberately reuse the f2b shuffled-control convention so
   510	   the programme's recovery statistics stay commensurable.
   511	
   512	---
   513	
   514	## 10. Registered assumption entries
   515	
   516	Fresh append-only block ASM-0960–0979 (this bead); entries 0967–0979 remain
   517	free.
   518	
   519	| Registered id | Scope |
   520	|---|---|
   521	| **ASM-0960** | DECONF contract: two-channel decomposition (runtime vs authored-content), stage inventory A1/A2/B with claim licences and gate placement, does-not-amend clauses, precursor/supersession relation to knull/knull-v2/f2b-transfer/RAGC/MF0 (§0, §7) |
   522	| **ASM-0961** | GS-A derivation rule: mechanical four-column projection of the kernel store onto the check() read-set; zero authored prose (hence outside the ASM-0700 quality-gate's object class); deterministic pinned script + content hash; coverage/alignment identity by construction (§2.1) |
   523	| **ASM-0962** | Stage A1 semantics: exhaustive decision-grid enumeration, C_dec counting statistic (no CI), the store-substitution invariance licensing rule at C_dec = 1.0, discordance classification + reachable-trajectory flag protocol, and the value-independent-of-surprise registration rationale (§3) |
   524	| **ASM-0963** | Stage A2: GS-C = a5 typed world in pinned SQLite + published CTE query library; endpoints A_exec + acc_generic on the pinned 977-query slice; 0.99 decision rule; anti-weak-control publication + grammar-conformance requirement (§4) |
   525	| **ASM-0964** | Stage B: arm set (R1-alone, GS-A-verify-retry, GR-A-lite, GR-D-lite, conditional kernel arm), external-gold scoring on the pinned d-adj-t labels, fresh-runs rule, right-sized parity adoption (token cap, generator parity, ledger rows), instrument + bridge gates, recovery_RAG/Δ_align endpoints with 0.70/0.30 rules, reporting requirements, compute caps + planning constants (§5, §8) |
   526	| **ASM-0965** | Verdict + wiring: three independent stage readings with equal-prominence deflationary outcomes and named rescue paths; the invariance-lemma rider rule incl. re-run-on-version-change; K-P3v2(4) input mapping at scope (§6, §7) |
   527	| **ASM-0966** | EXTRAPOLATION (non-load-bearing): expected A1 outcome C_dec = 1.0; resolved by the stage-A1 run itself |
   528	
   529	---
   530	
   531	## 11. Beads this design spawns (recommendation — the coordinator creates them; no bd operation is performed by this document)
   532	
   533	```
   534	P3-E-DECONF-0   [task, P0]  Freeze + run DECONF/1 stages A1/A2 (CPU, ~$0) per
   535	    this design after external review; emit the two verdict-inputs + the
   536	    invariance-lemma artifact (discordance file, store projection hash).
   537	    The steering already names this bead; stage A1 is the first
   538	    "cheap decisive kill" of the ratified next-wave list.
   539	P3-E-DECONF-B   [task, P1]  Prereg + freeze + run Stage B (single GPU run,
   540	    <= ~2 GPU-h) once A1 reads out; co-schedule with the f2b-transfer
   541	    stage-2 campaign; runner-role separation per the knull pattern.
   542	Dependency edges: P3-E-DECONF-B blocked-by P3-E-DECONF-0 (conditional arm
   543	    rule) + the d-adj-t label pin (already produced by f2b-transfer stage 1).
   544	```
   545	
   546	---
   547	
   548	## Epistemic register (what this design relied on)
   549	
   550	- **STIPULATED (registered block ASM-0960…ASM-0966):** every design choice
   551	  above; none is evidence about any store, engine, or thesis. Inherited binding
   552	  stipulations consumed: ASM-0853 (matched-retrieval rules), ASM-0922/0923/
   553	  0924/0925/0926 (RAGC mechanics, adopted right-sized), ASM-0891 (MF0 cell
   554	  ownership), ASM-0700/0703 (the knull plain-arm ruling, untouched), ASM-0812/
   555	  0813/0814 (controls, statistics, oracle labelling).
   556	- **MEASURED (restated strictly inside their envelopes):** f2b-replicate PASS
   557	  +0.1507 / LB +0.1053 / cost 0.103 / shuffled ~0 and its does_not_license
   558	  ceiling; the f2b-transfer stage-1 endorsement A = 0.9784 (LB 0.9606, gates
   559	  valid; artifact poc/f2b-transfer/judge-1-results/stage1-analysis.json,
   560	  results-log append pending); the knull M-V map 3456/3456; a5-llm PASS
   561	  +0.6602 with its no-conventional-substrate ceiling; the harness mechanism

   563	  (corpus-indexed, restated only as the mandatory disclosure).
   564	- **LIT-BACKED (through completed Phase-0 reviews):** BM25 as the mandatory
   565	  lexical baseline (BEIR, arXiv:2104.08663, NeurIPS 2021, via RAG.md §6.2);

   571	
   572	This document changes no frozen object, no verdict, no audit, no ruling; its

item.aggregated_output:
139	## 1. The mechanism fact the whole design leans on (stated once, precisely)
   140	
   141	For every arm f2b-replicate and f2b-transfer run or plan, the pipeline is a
   142	deterministic function of (models, items, seeds, store) in which the store is
   143	consulted ONLY through `check(record_extracted_from_answer, item)` — accept /
   144	reject / abstain — and through nothing else: no store bytes in any prompt, no
   145	store-dependent decoding, no store-dependent seed arithmetic [MEASURED:
   146	poc/f2b/runner/f2b_runner.py at the pinned f2b-replicate harness manifest —
   147	build_prompt takes (frames, item, context_docs) and the verify arms pass no
   148	context_docs; resampling is det_u over (arm, item id, seed, attempt) only].
   149	
   150	Consequence (a determinism statement, not a hypothesis): if a substitute store
   151	S′ yields the SAME check() decision as the kernel store on every (item,
   152	admissible answer) pair, then every output, every per-item correctness bit,
   153	every endpoint, and every verdict of every run of this topology — past AND
   154	future, membership-gold or external-gold — is invariant under kernel→S′
   155	substitution. The deconfound therefore does not need to re-run any model to
   156	decide the runtime channel: it needs to measure decision concordance over the
   157	FULL reachable decision space, which is finite and small (closed answer sets:
   158	≤4 options or yes/no per item). That is Stage A1.

   208	## 3. Stage A1 — the extensional-equivalence audit (the cheapest falsifier, ~$0, CPU)
   209	
   210	### 3.1 Procedure
   211	
   212	- DECISION: enumerate the FULL decision grid — for each of the three pinned
   213	  item corpora (d-qa 650, d-qa-r 1000, d-qa-t 360; corpus hashes as pinned in
   214	  the f2b-replicate / f2b-transfer records) × every admissible answer in the
   215	  item's closed answer space (each MC option; yes and no for claim items) ×
   216	  both verifier variants (true map; the seed-pinned shuffled derangement,
   217	  replayed with the same recorded permutation applied to GS-A rows) — compute
   218	  the decision triple (extract_ok, decidable, consistent) under (i) the pinned
   219	  KernelVerifier over the kernel store and (ii) the same checker class over
   220	  GS-A. Grid size ≤ ~16k decision pairs (2,010 items × ≤4 answers × 2
   221	  variants); pure string ops; minutes on the local box at nice -n 10
   222	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0962].
   223	
   224	### 3.2 Metric and decision rule
   225	
   226	- DECISION: the primary statistic is **C_dec = concordant decision triples /
   227	  total grid size** — an exhaustive COUNT over the full reachable decision
   228	  space, so it carries no sampling error and no confidence interval; the
   229	  pre-registered reading [STIPULATED: ASM-0962]:
   230	  - **C_dec = 1.0 ⇒ verdict-input KERNEL-RUNTIME-GENERIC (the deflationary
   231	    half).** By §1 determinism, the generic aligned answer-key store reproduces
   232	    every F2-line endpoint VERBATIM: the reproduction statistic the steering
   233	    asked for is `R_repro = lift(GS-A) / lift(kernel) ≡ 1.0` identically — the
   234	    measured +0.1507, its CIs, the shuffled-control readings, and every future
   235	    output of the same topology (including f2b-transfer stage-2's external-gold
   236	    endpoints when they run) are bit-for-bit invariant under kernel → GS-A
   237	    substitution. The kernel-SPECIFIC runtime claim on the F2 line is dead at
   238	    this scope, at zero model spend.
   239	  - **C_dec < 1.0 ⇒ a runtime kernel channel EXISTS.** Every discordant (item,
   240	    answer, variant) triple is emitted and classified by check-path (def-match
   241	    / term-match / claim / shuffled-composition); a discordance lying on a
   242	    trajectory reachable by any logged run additionally raises a flag on the
   243	    f2b-replicate interpretation (flag only — the frozen verdict is untouched).
   244	    The discordance classes become the measured definition of "what the kernel
   245	    semantics do at runtime" and Stage B adds the kernel-verify arm back
   246	    (§5.1's conditional arm) so the channel is priced end-to-end.
   247	- Falsifiability, stated honestly: C_dec = 1.0 is the EXPECTED outcome — the
   248	  strip is a projection onto check()'s read-set, and the knull M-V map already
   249	  measured the vector/AST fields inert (3456/3456) [MEASURED:
   250	  registry/experiments/knull.json map M-V]. The residual falsification room is
   251	  real but narrow: the term-for-definition path (`shown_definition`), the
   252	  claim-polarity membership path, the shuffled-map composition, and any
   253	  unnoticed engine consultation inside the checker would each surface as
   254	  discordance. The registered expectation is EXTRAPOLATION ASM-0966
   255	  (non-load-bearing, resolved by the run itself); the VALUE of the stage does
   256	  not depend on surprise — see §3.3.
   257	
   258	### 3.3 Why this is worth registering even if the outcome is expected
   259	
   260	The assessment's circularity reading is currently an ANALYTIC argument attached
   261	to a does_not_license note; programme discipline treats analytic readings and
   262	measured facts differently, and two external reviews have now demanded this
   263	deconfound be EXECUTED rather than argued (round1-steering item 5; the
   264	GPT-5.6 subjective analysis: "the cheap aligned-store control should not
   265	wait"). Stage A1 converts "a generic aligned answer-key WOULD reproduce it"
   266	into "a generic aligned answer-key DOES reproduce it, bit-for-bit, measured
   267	over the exhaustive decision space" — a registered, citable, audit-checkable
   268	fact that (a) every future F2-line claim must carry (§7), (b) re-scopes the
   269	headline language mechanically (§6), and (c) does so without spending the GPU
   270	budget, the human-annotation queue, or the knull quality-gate path that block
   271	every other deconfound [STIPULATED: ASM-0962].

    51	## 1. CORRECTNESS thesis — the verdict
    52	
    53	> **VERDICT (verbatim): INCONCLUSIVE — PENDING (a) an NL-reaching parser [FK-NLB-3] and (b) the semantics core [g2 Π-soundness vs human gold, g3 necessity]. The instrument sub-claim is FEASIBLE-and-DEMONSTRATED but INSTRUMENT-ONLY; every MEASURED crossing from the instrument into real or natural input is, so far, NEGATIVE. SCOPE: the kernel-v0 / molecules-v0 instance, R0–1.7B hosts, the two tested verticals (family/world, code), the one pinned a1-hybrid deterministic front-end, the self-authored covered slice. CONFIDENCE: high on the instrument sub-claim (cross-vendor CONFIRMED); high that the current-build NL boundary and wild-formal boundary are negative at scope; the thesis-level verdict is INCONCLUSIVE because its two decisive legs (semantics core, a better parser) are unrun, not failed.**
    54	
    55	The thesis decomposes into two claims that the evidence separates cleanly, and honesty
    56	requires keeping them apart:

     3	**What this is:** a synthesis of the two independent subjective analyses run at the batch-2 analysis
     4	phase — Fable (`round1-fable-subjective.md`) and GPT-5.6 (`poc/gpt56-review/round1-subjective/`). It
     5	REPORTS where the two models converge and diverge; it is OPINION/steering input (labelled as such per

     7	steer where effort goes next. The material redirection below is surfaced to the maintainer (issue #8).

     9	## Where the two models CONVERGE (high-confidence steering signals)
    10	
    11	1. **Scaffolding has outrun measurement.** GPT-5.6: Programme-3 "is in danger of becoming an
    12	   evaluation-and-governance programme with a neurosymbolic experiment attached… the documentation and
    13	   measurement superstructure are now far ahead of the experimental evidence." Fable: "since the NL
    14	   FAILs landed the programme has produced four review cycles of measurement scaffolding and **zero new
    15	   measurements against either wall**." → **Stop building scaffolding; start measuring.**
    16	
    17	2. **The portfolio is too broad — cut it.** Both say scarce attention is spread over too many
    18	   speculative families before the two existential questions are answered. Fable names the clearest
    19	   probable **dead ends**: H-DD dimension-drop ("dead several times over" — recommend DORMANT, cancel
    20	   design spend), general-NL GNN fusion (deltas shrink ~10× under LoRA-parity; causality probes show
    21	   models reading text not structure), deep-internal rule placements (delivery-without-integration).
    22	
    23	3. **Coverage is the existential risk, and the general-index ambition is probably already dead.**
    24	   Measured external coverage is ~zero everywhere it's been measured (0/1550; g8 0/1000). Fable: "the
    25	   honest normalised-scale G1 bound will likely kill every store-dependent family on any general index
    26	   … the general-index W1 ambition is probably already dead and the **vertical-index fork should be
    27	   pre-registered before the numbers force it**." GPT-5.6: "coverage can be grown" is meaningless
    28	   without bytes and authoring cost.
    29	
    30	4. **Attack the NL wall NOW, and make it THE main experiment.** Both: the design respects the wall
    31	   (NLB gates everything) but the actual attack hasn't started — "building the courtroom before running
    32	   the cheap forensic tests" (Fable) / "in design terms yes; in resource allocation and experimental
    33	   urgency, no" (GPT-5.6). NLB should receive most of the next wave's attention, not be one gated
    34	   workstream.
    35	
    36	5. **The single biggest risk (both, nearly verbatim):** the kernel's distinctive semantics contribute
    37	   nothing beyond what a generic typed store + executor + strong tool/RAG baseline already provide. →
    38	   the **aligned-generic-store deconfound** is the highest-value early experiment.
    39	
    40	6. **The single most promising direction (both converge):** a **narrow structured-domain (e.g. code)
    41	   selective coprocessor** — deterministic extraction + a learned contract front-end that produces an
    42	   ambiguity set + exact execution + bounded verify-retry / span-scoped speculative decoding — and
    43	   demand a matched end-to-end win THERE, where syntax + deterministic extraction + clarification make
    44	   the NL boundary tractable.
    45	
    46	## Divergence
    47	Minimal. The two analyses are unusually aligned. Fable adds the most explicit "kill list" (H-DD DORMANT,
    48	NTP engine-trace gate, freeze scaffolding until a G2 result) and flags the a5-llm audit-ledger
    49	contradiction; GPT-5.6 adds the sharpest framing of "delivery≠use / a correct checker cannot repair a
    50	wrong formalization" and the positive-value abstention concern. No contradiction between them.
    51	
    52	## Recommended steering for the next wave (pending maintainer ratification, issue #8)
    53	- **Freeze new measurement-scaffolding design.** No more MF0/FRONT/ORACLE-class docs until a family
    54	  produces a G2 (real-input) result.
    55	- **Cut / DORMANT:** H-DD dimension-drop; deprioritise broad general-NL GNN fusion and deep-internal
    56	  rule placements. Redirect that Fable spend to measurement.
    57	- **Run the cheap decisive kills now (all ~$0 / CPU):** (a) the coverage-ceiling paper-kill (POWER rig,
    58	  `poc/p3-power/`) on the existing censuses; (b) the aligned-generic-store deconfound; (c) the NTP
    59	  "can the engine actually emit continuation sets / traces" gate; (d) stand up the real held-out NLB
    60	  gate on the code vertical.
    61	- **Pre-register the vertical (code) fork** as the primary W1 target; treat the general index as a
    62	  stretch/secondary.
    63	- **Human-annotation = P0 workstream** (the human-eval workbook is now delivered — aligned).
    64	
    65	Concrete next beads to create once ratified: P3-E-POWER-0 (coverage-ceiling run), P3-E-DECONF-0
    66	(aligned-generic-store deconfound), P3-E-NTP-GATE-0 (engine-trace emission), P3-D-NLB → P3-E-NLB-1 on
    67	the code vertical; mark H-DD/SURG-design DORMANT.

    29	  is never achievable savings.

    33	## 0. The one sentence that still governs everything
    34	
    35	LOAD-BEARING: Across the entire registry there are ZERO audited end-task wins over the kernel-as-text null that are attributable to the kernel's content, and the question the whole programme exists to answer — *does grounding a model in the kernel make that model measurably more correct or more efficient at matched budget, on real input?* — remains, as of 2026-07-11, UNSETTLED on both theses [MEASURED: registry/assessments/oracle-coverage.json null-bound + registry/assessments/f-efficiency.json — every end-task-adjacent verdict is either R0 (no host model), a landed FAIL, or a PASS confined to a self-authored oracle-favourable *formal* slice whose content-attribution is confounded].
    36	
    37	The single most robust cross-experiment fact, and the one that shapes both verdicts:
    38	
    39	LOAD-BEARING (direction-only): the kernel's deterministic machinery is EXACT and sound *inside its own closed grammar / self-authored substrate*, and every MEASURED crossing of a boundary into real or natural input either FAILs or is INCONCLUSIVE [EXTRAPOLATION: ASM-0762, extending ASM-0721; each figure is index-bound to its own verdict's envelope and this cross-reading is never a premise — machinery side l3a/a5 gold-replication identical 527/527 & 855/855, g8 F/B round-trip 33/33; boundary side (i) NL input l3a-parse 47.6% / a5-nl 41.6% both FAIL, (ii) wild-formal g8 0/1000 FAIL, (iii) model-internal nsk1 INCONCLUSIVE, (iv) compute-matched verifier-offload f2 FAIL].

    41	I call this shape the **reachability-wall-on-coverage-wall**: there is a *coverage* wall
    42	(does the kernel even contain the target — g8 0/1000, m0b 0.3542, compression-census
    43	~0.6%, b-cov-define-lane 0/1,550) and, stacked on top of it, a *reachability* wall (even
    44	for content the kernel demonstrably CONTAINS and CAN decide perfectly under gold parse,
    45	natural language cannot reliably ADDRESS it through the pinned deterministic front-end).
    46	The programme is much further along on *building a defensible checker* than on *showing
    47	the checker reaches, or helps, anyone*.

   133	## 0. What Programme-3 is, and what it inherits
   134	
   135	The maintainer's intent, restated: use something *like* the kernel + world
   136	information this programme has built to create **neurosymbolic AI architectures**
   137	that (1) achieve a **higher AI index than comparably-resourced models**, and then
   138	(2) find how **small** the whole system (model + kernel + world + everything else)
   139	can be made while staying competitive on that index. Because the system is
   140	neurosymbolic, size cannot be parameters: size is **deployment bytes** plus a full
   141	**inference resource vector**. Start very small to learn cheaply which
   142	architectures work; then continuously scale while improving them. (The review is
   143	right that the literal "higher than ALL other models of the same size and compute"
   144	is not a testable claim; §1.4 narrows what W1 actually licenses — the maintainer's
   145	north-star is preserved as the *aspiration*, the testable claim is the frontier
   146	statement.)
   147

   149	instruments, seams, and — critically — its measured walls. The honest starting
   150	position, restated from the capstone with its own tags:

   300	  working set + total bytes read to first answer; (5) index/store construction
   301	  size and construction cost (feeds KOT-LIFE/1); (6) any remote service, cache, or

   311	There is no "the kernel is free" accounting, ever. Quantisation games are

   314	
   315	### 1.3 Inference cost — KOT-COST/2 (the resource vector) + the neural-FLOP diagnostic
   316	
   317	The review's technical error #7 is accepted: a ledger in which symbolic
   318	computation enters at zero is not a compute measure — a system doing substantial
   319	CPU search, retrieval, decompression, or I/O could look "compute-matched" by
   320	definition. Revision 1's L1 is therefore RENAMED and DEMOTED to what it actually
   321	is, and the binding ledger becomes a full resource vector [STIPULATED: ASM-0810].
   322	
   323	- DECISION: inference cost is the measured **resource vector KOT-COST/2**, per
   324	  query and per suite, on pinned hardware under the P3-D-HW repeatability
   325	  protocol: (a) accelerator time + estimated accelerator ops; (b) CPU-seconds;
   326	  (c) bytes read from storage and network; (d) energy (RAPL on CPU,
   327	  NVML/`nvidia-smi`-class counters on GPU, whole-run integration — not query-level
   328	  spot reads); (e) peak accelerator memory AND host RSS; (f) end-to-end latency at
   329	  p50 and p95; (g) throughput under a pinned concurrency distribution; (h) warm-
   330	  and cold-cache conditions both measured, including startup/index-load time;
   331	  (i) time-to-first-token and inter-token latency for generative arms; (j) output
   332	  length controlled (pinned max-token + stop discipline) or cost normalised per
   333	  emitted token, pinned per benchmark. Methodology borrows MLPerf inference +
   334	  power measurement discipline (REVIEW-CITED: MLPerf inference-datacenter
   335	  benchmarks + MLPerf power methodology; verified and operationalised by
   336	  P3-LR-SYS → P3-D-HW before the framework freezes) [STIPULATED: ASM-0810].
   337	- DECISION: the analytic neural-FLOP ledger survives as the **neural-FLOP
   338	  diagnostic** (2·params·tokens + attention terms, pinned formula sheet): useful
   339	  to keep the neural parts honest and hardware-independent, REPORTED always,
   340	  BINDING never — no "compute-matched" claim may cite it alone [STIPULATED:
   341	  ASM-0810; corrects technical error #7].
   342	
   343	Precedent that the symbolic side can be genuinely cheap on the measured vector —
   344	and that the accounting has to be measured, not assumed:
   345	
   346	- PREMISE: the deterministic engine answers covered queries at 5.29–7.82 µs/query,
   347	  and the f2b system-level cost ratio vs the 1.7B host was measured at 0.103
   348	  [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/l3a.json + registry/verdicts/a5.json engine timing;
   349	  registry/verdicts/f2b-replicate.json cost_ratio_vs_R3].

   351	### 1.3a The lifecycle ledger — KOT-LIFE/1
   352	
   353	The comparison this programme runs is a *deployment-efficiency* comparison, not an
   354	equal-total-investment comparison — legitimate, but it must be named accurately
   355	and priced [STIPULATED: ASM-0810, answering review "add a lifecycle ledger"].
   356	
   357	- DECISION: every W1-relevant system (S and every comparator) publishes a
   358	  **lifecycle ledger**: (1) donor pretraining provenance and compute where known
   359	  (dimension-drop arms *inherit* their donor's training compute — stated, never
   360	  netted out); (2) architecture fine-tuning + hyperparameter-search compute
   361	  actually spent (the tuning-symmetry audit trail, §2.3); (3) store costs:
   362	  authoring, parsing, embedding, indexing, and human review, in hours and $;
   363	  (4) total cost of ownership amortised at three pinned deployment volumes —
   364	  10^3, 10^6, 10^9 queries — using the measured KOT-COST/2 energy/latency figures.
   365	  Training longer to obtain a smaller model is a real deployment strategy and
   366	  optimal size depends on inference volume (REVIEW-CITED: Sardana et al., "Beyond
   367	  Chinchilla-Optimal"; verified by P3-LR-SYS). The ledger CO-REPORTS with every W1
   368	  claim and feeds kill condition K-P3v2(6); it does not gate W1 numerically
   369	  because store-authoring hours and GPU-hours have no non-arbitrary common unit —
   370	  the honesty mechanism is mandatory publication + the kill condition, not a
   371	  fudged exchange rate [STIPULATED: ASM-0810].

   373	### 1.4 The exact win condition and the exact shrink objective
   374	
   375	The revision-1 ±10% band construction is WITHDRAWN as gameable (padding into a
   376	favourable band; dodging a strong model just outside it; empty band
   377	intersections; losing to a smaller/cheaper dominator while "winning the
   378	neighbourhood") [STIPULATED: ASM-0810, accepting review ranked concern #1 in
   379	full].
   380	
   381	**W1 (the resource-frontier competitive claim), verbatim:** at rung R-k, with a
   382	pre-registered resource budget vector
   383	`B_k = (max canonically-packed deployment bytes; max p95 latency; max energy/query;
   384	max peak accelerator memory + host RSS)`, a Programme-3 system S beats the
   385	baseline frontier iff, under the frozen KOT-AI-INDEX/2 version for that rung,
   386	own-harness:
   387	
   388	1. **Admissibility:** S fits within EVERY component of B_k, measured under
   389	   KOT-SIZE/2 + KOT-COST/2, warm and cold conditions per the P3-D-HW protocol
   390	   (energy explicitly binds here — fixing revision 1's omission of energy from
   391	   W1 while KOT-COST claimed it); and
   392	2. **Frontier dominance:** for EVERY comparator C in the pre-registered comparator
   393	   set F(B_k) — every pre-registered, reproducible open comparator and baseline
   394	   family that fits within every component of B_k, explicitly INCLUDING systems
   395	   smaller or cheaper than S, each optimised for the budget by the frontier-builder
   396	   (§2.3) — the simultaneous one-sided 95% lower confidence bound satisfies
   397	   `LCB95(INDEX(S) − INDEX(C)) > δ_k`, with family-wise error control across the
   398	   comparator set and a hierarchical bootstrap over benchmark families and items
   399	   preserving paired predictions (§2.5); and
   400	3. the decontamination audit (§2.2) AND the sealed-evaluation consistency gate
   401	   (§2.2a) pass; and
   402	4. the factorial controls (§2.2) behave as pre-registered (derangement/permutation
   403	   destroys any store-content-attributed component; the aligned-non-kernel and
   404	   matched-generic-RAG results are REPORTED as the attribution split, whatever
   405	   they show); and
   406	5. the lifecycle ledger (§1.3a) is published alongside.
   407	
   408	Reporting: the full **staircase/Pareto envelope** of neural and neural-retrieval
   409	points across budgets (index vs bytes, index vs cost) is published with S plotted
   410	against it — W1 means S lies significantly above the envelope at its budget, not
   411	that it won a favourable neighbourhood.
   412	
   413	**Scope of the claim licensed by a W1 PASS, verbatim:** "S exceeds every
   414	pre-registered, reproducible open comparator and baseline family searched under
   415	budget B_k by ≥ δ_k on KOT-AI-INDEX/2-vN" — NEVER "higher than all other models of
   416	the same size and compute"; the literal universal is untestable and is hereby
   417	retired from every claim surface [STIPULATED: ASM-0810].
   418	
   419	**W2 (the shrink objective), verbatim:** once W1 first holds at any rung, minimise
   420	KOT-SIZE/2 figure-(1) bytes subject to `INDEX(S) ≥ INDEX(M_ref)` where M_ref is a
   421	named, pinned open reference model (initial M_ref = SmolLM2-1.7B own-harness;
   422	revisited by maintainer fork per index version). The deliverable is the measured
   423	**Pareto frontier over (bytes, resource vector, index)**; headline success = at
   424	least one point that strictly dominates every measurable open point at the small
   425	end. Consistency rule (fixing the review's W2 finding): the R-2 sweep inside
   426	P3-E-SCALE-2 runs ONLY conditional on W1 passing at R-2; any compression curve
   427	produced before a W1 PASS (e.g. H-DD dose-response curves) is labelled
   428	**DIAGNOSTIC**, never W2 [STIPULATED: ASM-0817].
   429	
   430	Falsifiability: W1 is falsified per-rung by failing its own margin against the
   431	frontier; the programme has quantitative kill conditions in §7. There is no
   432	"moral victory" clause: frontier parity is a FAIL of W1, however interesting the
   433	architecture.
